headline: McCormick Weighs Unilever Food Merger As Valuation Signals Potential Upside
summary: McCormick (NYSE:MKC) is in advanced talks to merge with Unilever's food division in an all stock deal. The proposed combination would add brands such as Hellmann's and Knorr to McCormick's portfolio. Both companies have confirmed discussions, which are ongoing and not yet finalized. For investors watching McCormick at a share price of $54.05, this potential merger follows a period of weaker stock performance, with the company showing a 31.3% decline over the past year and a 32.4% decline...

headline: Why Mayo and Soap No Longer Mix for Unilever
summary: For almost 100 years, the answer for consumer-goods behemoth  Unilever  was yes.  Unilever said Friday it was in talks with spice maker  McCormick  about a sale of its food business, which includes Hellmann’s mayonnaise and Knorr stock cubes.  A sale would mark the biggest portfolio shake-up for Unilever since it was created nearly a century ago through the merger of Dutch group Margarine Unie and British soap maker Lever Bros. It would also be the company’s most significant move yet to shed slower-growing food brands and push deeper into health and hygiene products.

A sale would mark the biggest portfolio shake-up for Unilever since it was created nearly a century ago through the merger of Dutch group Margarine Unie and British soap maker Lever Bros. It would also be the company’s most significant move yet to shed slower-growing food brands and push deeper into health and hygiene products.

headline: Unilever, McCormick Deal Would Create a New Condiment King
summary: A tie-up between mayonnaise-maker Unilever and McCormick, which sells Cholula hot sauce, would anoint a new sauce boss at a time when consumers are condiment obsessed.  The union could “completely transform” McCormick’s business, bringing the spice maker increased scale, international reach, and sway with retailers, according to TD Cowen analyst Rob Moskow.  McCormick has fared better than many other big food companies in recent years, posting growing sales volumes at a time when others are struggling with slumping revenues.

headline: Unilever in talks to sell foods business to U.S.-based buyer
summary: STORY: Unilever is in talks to sell its foods business to the U.S.-based McCormick & Company.A possible deal would bring together Unilever brands like Hellmann's with McCormick's famous Cholula hot sauce.Unilever's foods segment made up about a quarter of its total sales last year - which made it just under $15 billion.The firm confirmed Friday (March 20) it had received an inbound offer for the business.The talks signal a potential speed up of CEO Fernando Fernandez's new strategy.He wants to reposition Unilever towards higher-margin beauty and personal care categories.The conglomerate already sold-off its ice cream business last year, which included the Magnum brand.The companies said in a statement there's no certainty they will agree a deal.They also didn't give out financial details.Unilever's foods business includes brands like Colman and Marmite.

raw.headline: Unilever in talks to sell foods business to U.S.-based buyer
raw.summary: STORY: Unilever is in talks to sell its foods business to the U.S.-based McCormick & Company.A possible deal would bring together Unilever brands like Hellmann's with McCormick's famous Cholula hot sauce.Unilever's foods segment made up about a quarter of its total sales last year - which made it just under $15 billion.The firm confirmed Friday (March 20) it had received an inbound offer for the business.The talks signal a potential speed up of CEO Fernando Fernandez's new strategy.He wants to reposition Unilever towards higher-margin beauty and personal care categories.The conglomerate already sold-off its ice cream business last year, which included the Magnum brand.The companies said in a statement there's no certainty they will agree a deal.They also didn't give out financial details.Unilever's foods business includes brands like Colman and Marmite.

headline: Unilever Shareholders Smile on Potential Deal to Separate Food Business
summary: Unilever shares rose in European trading after the consumer-products giant confirmed it was in talks to separate its food business and combine it with spice maker McCormick.  Unilever Chief Executive Fernando Fernandez said in February that the maker of Dove soap and Hellmann’s mayonnaise was in no rush to divest the food business, which he said was outperforming the broader food sector.  A deal with McCormick would leave Unilever focused on personal-care products and home.

headline: Unilever Confirms Talks on McCormick Offer for Food Business
summary: The Anglo-Dutch consumer goods company said Friday it had received an offer from the Maryland-based spices and seasonings maker, but that there was no certainty a deal would be achieved.  Barclays estimates Unilever’s food division has an equity value of some €28 billion ($32.4 billion) to €31 billion.  A sale would mark the end of Unilever competing with Big Food rivals like Kraft Heinz Co., Nestlé SA and PepsiCo Inc., and transform the multinational into a major household and personal care company on par with L’Oréal SA, Beiersdorf AG and Estée Lauder Cos.

Barclays estimates Unilever’s food division has an equity value of some €28 billion ($32.4 billion) to €31 billion.  A sale would mark the end of Unilever competing with Big Food rivals like Kraft Heinz Co., Nestlé SA and PepsiCo Inc., and transform the multinational into a major household and personal care company on par with L’Oréal SA, Beiersdorf AG and Estée Lauder Cos.

headline: Unilever Food Split Talks With McCormick Reshape Focus And Valuation Gap
summary: Unilever is reportedly in advanced talks to separate its food division and explore an all stock merger with McCormick. The potential deal would refocus Unilever on beauty and personal care while creating a new packaged foods player. The transaction is described as under serious discussion and could be announced within weeks. For investors watching LSE:ULVR, this reported move comes as the shares trade around £45.74, with a 15.5% decline over the past month and a 5.3% decline over the past...
